object_listened_to: Distorted protest recording from an unknown city

mode_chain.reason: Evidence and harm terms require forensic-archival caution.
Crowd voices and speech require separation of vocal sound, transcript, identity, and model mediation.
Protest, policing, and platform stakes require institutional and harm-context caution.
Technical inspection becomes available only if the actual audio file is supplied.

agent_handoff.summary: Route the described protest recording through forensic-archival-listening (primary) and voice-speech-listening (secondary), with critical-political-listening as corrective; evidence level is prompt_only and route confidence is low because the description competes between evidentiary, voice, and political frames.
required_inputs: the actual audio file or verified metadata
recording chain, custody, and upload context
corroborating context for event, location, and time
forbidden_assumptions: Do not identify source, intention, identity, culture, or event sequence without evidence.
Do not move theory, affect, or culture into inferred claims.
Do not move speculation outside the speculative category.
Do not claim measured audio traits from text-only input.
Do not treat transcript text as the whole voice.

stop_conditions: Stop if the named evidence cannot be accessed; never substitute imagined evidence.
Stop before any measured claim until audio, metadata, or measured signal data is supplied.
Stop before identity, location, or event-sequence claims without corroborating context.
Stop before identity, emotion, or consent claims based on voice alone.
Stop and request the object, input type, or user intent before deep analysis; route confidence is low.
